title: chlorophora tinctoria var. ovata
intro: Chlorophora tinctoria var. ovata, also known as the Indian mulberry or the Butea tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, known for its vibrant red flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Chlorophora tinctoria var. ovata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include a tea made from the leaves and flowers, as well as a dye extracted from the flowers, which has been used for centuries to create a range of colors from pink to red.
